com.salvation.coremod.gui.environmental_: Place a beacon core on top of 4 Tier 3+ building materials to form a Purifying Beacon.
There are signs when the barrier against corruption is impacted. A sickly green means decay, a glowing white means a degree of repair.
This sickness we have discovered is a form of corruption, seeping into our world from somewhere else.
We are calling this other dimension Exteritio. Little is known about it yet, other than that its voracious appetite to consume is reaching towards us.
With a purifying furnace the essence of corruption can be extracted from corrupted items and then used by purification beacons to fight back against corruption.
Left unchecked, corruption can cause livestock to become dangerous, crops to fail and even the land and trees to mutate.
A corruption extractor can remove corruption from affected animals and plants.
Voraxium ore is created by thin spots between the Overworld and Exteritio. It can be dangerous unless purified.
A portal to Exteritio can be created from neutralized blightwood, activated with a Purified Iron tool.
A Voraxian Locator is sensitive to corruption, changes color based on local severity, and can be used to detect the source.
com.salvation.gui.modules.special_resear:
When not actively advancing specific research, researchers investigate the corruption, building up a balance of Research Credits (ƒ). These credits can be used to advance specific research to better understand the corruption and its effects.

The Lab Tech also contributes research credits by purifying corrupted items.

message.salvation.corruption.stage_1_nor: A faint corruption stirs in the world. A sign of thinning barriers against an inimical threat.
message.salvation.corruption.stage_2_awa: The corruption has a stronger foothold in the overworld, and signs of its impact are visible.
message.salvation.corruption.stage_3_spr: Barriers against the corruption have weakened, and the corruption is spreading.
message.salvation.corruption.stage_4_dan: Tendrils of corruption from Exteritio now connect to the Overworld, and the danger is growing.
message.salvation.corruption.stage_5_cri: The corruption has become critical, and the Overworld is in danger. Perhaps the battle needs to be taken to the source.
message.salvation.corruption.stage_6_ter: The corruption is at the pinnacle of its power, and the world isn't safe. You may need to find and eliminate it at its source.
message.salvation.corruption.stage_downw: The threat of corruption subsides somewhat...
message.salvation.voraxian_locator.wrong: The locator cannot find Exteritio from here.
message.salvation.voraxian_locator.no_ta: The locator cannot stabilize on a target.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.1: Nature's beauty brings a sense of peace.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.2: The land rests easily beneath your feet.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.3: The world feels strong and well-rested.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.4: The animals move with quiet confidence.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.5: The air feels clean and pure.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.6: The sky is calm and untroubled.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.7: Plants grow as they always have.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage0.9: The roots below feel deep and secure.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.1: The land feels strained, as if too much is asked of it.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.2: The world is starting to feel tired.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.4: The air feels thicker than it should, as if under pressure from some outside force...
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.5: Somewhere in the distance a forlorn howl echoes, sending shivers down your spine.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.6: The plants seem stunted and unhealthy.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.7: The clouds feel thick and sickly.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage1.9: You can't shake the feeling of being watched.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.0: The land groans under unseen weight.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.1: The world is exhausted. It can't maintain its protective barriers the way it once could.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.2: Animals avoid places they once trusted.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.4: Some plants refuse to grow at all.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.5: The wind carries an anxious tone.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.6: The sky feels distant and cold.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage2.7: The soil no longer feels alive.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage3.7: This place doesn't remember how it used to be.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage3.9: Something is interfering with the natural order.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage4.0: The land is fevered - as if fighting off an infection..
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage4.3: What grows here does not feel healthy.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage4.4: Every sound feels like a warning.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage4.7: This place craves peace, but can find none.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage4.8: The world feels angry and embattled.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage4.9: Environmental collapse isn't inevitable - it is a product of carelessness.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage5.1: Creatures move as if driven by fear.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage5.3: Every blooming flower fights back against the corruption.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage5.4: The world cries out in pain.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage5.5: The sky feels wrong in every way.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage5.6: The ground no longer supports life.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage5.9: Corruption feeds on life and gives nothing back.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage6.1: Nothing here behaves as it should.
com.salvation.flavormessage.stage6.9: There is no tunnel; much less a light at the end; just stifling darkness.

com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.0: I feel safe working this land.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.1: There's a calm here that makes the work easier.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.2: The soil feels good beneath my boots today.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.3: Everything around us feels strong and healthy.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.4: The animals don't seem afraid of us here.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.5: The air feels clean. I could breathe it all day.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.6: I like it here. The world feels steady.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.7: The crops are growing just the way they should.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.8: It feels like the land itself is helping us.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage0.9: I sleep well knowing the land is healthy.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.0: Have you noticed something feels a little... off lately?
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.1: The land feels tired, like it needs a rest.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.2: My work feels harder than it used to.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.3: The animals have been acting strange.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.5: I keep hearing distant noises that make my skin crawl.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.6: Some of the plants just aren't growing right.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.7: The sky looks heavier than usual.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.8: I can't quite explain it, but the ground feels unsettled.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage1.9: Sometimes it feels like something is watching us.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.0: Something is wrong with the land, I can feel it.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.1: Everything feels strained, like the world is exhausted.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.2: Animals are avoiding places they used to roam.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.3: Breathing doesn't feel as easy as it once did.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.4: Some crops just refuse to grow anymore.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.6: The sky feels distant and cold lately.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.7: The soil doesn't feel alive like it used to.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.8: It's too quiet out there sometimes.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage2.9: I worry something bigger is going wrong.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.0: This place feels like it doesn't want us here anymore.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.1: The animals behave in ways I've never seen before.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.3: The plants grow, but something about them feels unnatural.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.4: Even the quiet here makes me uneasy.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.5: The sky feels like it's pressing down on us.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.6: The ground feels sick beneath my feet.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.7: It's like the land forgot what it used to be.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.8: Everything here feels under terrible strain.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage3.9: Something is disturbing the natural order.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.0: The land feels feverish, like it's fighting something.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.1: Creatures around here are far more aggressive now.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.2: The air burns in my lungs sometimes.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.3: The plants that grow here don't look healthy anymore.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.4: Every sound makes me think danger is near.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.5: The sky feels like it's watching us.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.6: The ground itself seems to reject life.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.7: This place feels like it's desperate for peace.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.8: The world feels angry and wounded.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage4.9: We did something to this land... and now it fights back.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.0: The land is falling apart around us.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.1: Even the animals move like they're terrified.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.3: Nothing grows here without something going wrong.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.4: It feels like the world is crying out in pain.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.5: The sky feels wrong every time I look up.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.6: The ground barely supports life anymore.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.7: Decay spreads faster than anything can grow.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.8: I fear this place is failing.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage5.9: Whatever this corruption is, it only takes.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.0: The land is dying, and I don't know how to stop it.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.1: Nothing here behaves the way it should.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.3: Life here is barely holding on.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.4: It feels like the world is screaming and no one can hear it.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.5: The sky gives no comfort anymore.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.6: The ground feels hollow and dead.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.7: This place is beyond balance now.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.8: Something terrible has happened to the world.
com.salvation.citizenmessage.stage6.9: If this continues, I don't think anything will survive.
com.minecolonies.coremod.gui.townhall.ha: The colony's efforts at sustainable living.
com.salvation.coremod.gui.chat.recruitst: We ignored the disturbances. Sickly green clouds when we chopped down trees. Turns out we were eroding our own defenses.
Do you ever notice that the world sparkles when you plant a flower? It wants that flower there.
I dream of big eyes. They watch me from the dark. They step through my nightmares and into my home. I can't sleep.
I was a butcher. It needs to be done, you know. Now I know how the pig feels. I had to leave my old town. Can't feel like the pig all the time.
Call me a tree hugger, I don't care. They protect us. They protect us from the corruption. You can tell when you plant a tree the world is happy.
Digging, digging, always digging. Mostly fine, you know? But sometimes you know you've scraped away a little bit of that barrier. Barrier to what? Someplace else...
My old town was trying hard... the environmental lab though it had a solution - but I couldn't wait. I needed to find a healthier place to live.
Do you ever wonder what else is out there? I do. I think I'm the only one who can see it. I'm the only one who can see the truth. I'm hiding here.
Take away all the trees in a place and of course it will be empty. But I don't want empty places, I want places full of life. I want a place that still feels normal.

You know that odd state between fully asleep and fully awake? When your imagination runs wild, and you can't tell what's real? There's an entire separate reality right there waiting for us - trying to get in.
Do you have researchers? An environmental lab? My last town didn't take the threat seriously, but I'm scared...
Saplings changed, food changed... Eventually we figured out how to extract the corruption. Wish I'd brough a Corruption Extractor with me, just in case.
The crops looked fine, but they didn't feel right somehow. Hard to explain.
I had trouble sleeping back home. Everyone felt that way. Our Environmental Lab was busy looking for a cure, but I couldn't wait.
Animals were acting skittish. I swear I saw one get up on its hind legs and walk. Crazy look in its eyes. I don't know what's going on.
I like quiet places, but my village got TOO quiet. Suspiciously so. It's not like that here, is it?
The Purification Beacons went up too late to save us. They helped push back the corruption but it was too little, too late.
Everyone said things were fine. I wasn't so sure. Some of the creatures in the dark... they didn't look right.
Burning stuff gave off these noxious green clouds. The environmental lab developed something better, but I don't know how they did it.
You just can't grow faster than the land can support. We made that mistake at my old place. Don't make that mistake here! Get someone to research that - trust me.
Back home, the land looked tired. Like it needed a long rest. Blight on the grass, blight in the trees.
We worked harder every season, but got less for it. Our scientists said they had a cure, but the politicians never listened. I had to leave.
Some animals wouldn't eat, others wouldn't stop. You ever seen a pig stand up and stare you in the eye like YOU were bacon? Not for me.
The soil kept crumbling in my hands. Never used to do that. Bereft of life. Sucked dry.
I heard stories of places where the land still cooperates. Is this one of them? Maybe I'd like to stay.
I kept saying 'You cannot keep taking taking taking and never give!' Nature don't work like that. Who listened?  Nobody.
Unfettered capitalism relies on constant extraction. It thrives on keeping people desparate. It thrives on corruption.
I left when the livestock started changing their routines. Lab Tech said he could extract the corruption. I'll believe it when I see it.
The nights grew longer. Or maybe it just felt that way. When we raised the Purification Beacons it was too late to push back the night.
We pull things out of the ground willy-nilly. Well, that damages the Overworld. And some of that stuff - it's just unstable. Dangerous to use.
I watched a field go barren in a single season. Corruption throughout. Rotten to the core. I need help.  Will I find it here?
Some animals were born wrong. Not wrong like 'poor guy has three legs' but WRONG - like 'dang cow tried to eat me'.
The ground cracked where it never had before. A stench wafted up and corrupted water poured forth. Filth. Filth everywhere.
Roots was our head ecologist. Always said 'The land always asks for help, and in our town we listen!' But when Roots died, people stopped listening. Things got bad.
Conspiracy theorist? Not me. The corruption DOES seep in through thin spots to a corrupted land. We DO make it worse by our own actions. Don't look at me like that...
I heard whispers of places the land itself rejected. Sucked it through a hole to somewhere else. Nightmare fuel. I don't want to go there.
Water pooled where it shouldn't, dark and rippling with ill intent. It left an oily film on all it touched. I still smell it in my worst dreams.
Something is spreading. Slowly, but surely. Step by step. Cut tree by cut tree. Cloud of coalsmoke by cloud of coalsmoke. I'm on the run. I hope you can stop it.
My town is dying. I need help. I need it now. The thinner the barrier gets the more the Voraxians reach through that barrier. They could even show up here...
I'd rather help build something than watch it decay. Put me to work. Research? Environmental Lab? Something that does good.
We started marking places on maps. Places you don't go anymore. Weird shimmering portals anchored in neutralized blightwood.
Animals attacked without hunger or fear. Stood right up on two legs and attacked! Story goes that Aemul Benny got EATEN!
The land itself felt hostile underfoot. I don't mean strong glare and a judgy sniff - I mean strike-you-down-where-you-stand hostile!
Some nights the sky looked bruised. Researchers kept telling us to burn this newfangled purification fuel, but nobody did. It was more expensive!
*heheheh* OOOH! *mmmmm?* NO NO NO NO! What? it's here? IT'S HERE!? Don't breathe the green clouds...
We were so close. We invested in science. We built an environmental laboratory. We just started too late. Don't make the same mistake!
You see the green gas? Comes out when you burn? Comes out when you mine? Comes out as through the connections... Smells evil. Gotta fight it.
We kept records. Every furnace burn, every tree felled, every vein mined. The numbers climbed. The land responded. We did this.
They told us corruption was natural. Cycles. Balance. Funny how it only grew when we expanded carelessly.
There are chunks where compasses spin and animals refuse to spawn. Black zones. We pretend they don't exist.
The purification fuel worked. Slowed things down. But slowing isn't stopping, is it?
We lost people to things that used to be animals. We lost people to things that used to be trees. We lost people to things that used to be us.
There are holes... in the ground. They shimmer. They whisper. They grow. They grow. They grow...
When every decision is designed to maximize profit, only the richest survive. But even they can't overcome the corruption with money alone.
I was a scientist? I AM a scientist! Everything is fixable - even corruption! Observe, orient, do, act. Rinse, repeat. It's the only way.
I heard your colony was resisting. A bastion of strength. A beacon in the night. Some bacon in the pan... Tell me there's room for me?
They laughed at me. 'Voraxians?', they said. And laughed. Who is laughing now?  Well, not me either, really... It's too much.
You can smell corruption before you see it. Like burned coal and rot. Running only delays the inevitable.
*singing* No distance too great. No distance to regret. Not a stance to forget. No dance but death. *sighs*
This feels like the last place that matters. Does it matter enough? Does it matter at all?
The world is dying. I won't pretend otherwise. Let's stop whining about it and take this fight to the source!
Corruption took my home. I won't let it take everything. I won't let it take me.
I've seen what happens when it wins. We can't let it win - even if we have to take the fight to them.
Scars are not just the mark of time, they are the mark of violence. Violence against our land. Violence against us.
We discovered the truth too late. A parallel universe ravaged by unquenchable desire for more. More resources, more power. More... more... more... They have nothing left and now eye us.
Billionaires gesture and the world genuflects. Every depravity, every sin, every befouled river - I lay this at their feet.
I don't expect safety. I expect purpose. I expect action. I expect change. Can you give me that?
We either fight the corruption... or become part of it. Our actions can slow it, but only by taking the fight to them can we defeat it.
Cut down a tree? Plant a new tree! It's not that hard. The world naturally fights back against corruption - we're just stripping away the defenses.
Sometimes I think there is no tunnel, much less a light at the end... just stifling darkness. Tell me things are better here.
